animal_model: Female thymus free nude mice aged 4-5 weeks (nu/nu) implanted MDA-MB-435 estrogen receptor negative human breast cancer cells into the mouse mammary gland fat pad.
cell_lines: MDA-MB-435 estrogen receptor negative human breast cancer cells
excretion: The metabolism study on [99mTc(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)] was performed using the tumor- bearing mice. Since it is excreted via both renal and hepato-biliary routes, we collected samples from urine and feces to determine if they are able to retain their chemical integrity at 2 h postinjection. Figure 6 sowed typical radio-HPLC chromato- grams of [99mTc(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)] in the kit matrix, urine, and feces at 120 min postinjection. About 80% metabolite of [99mTc(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)] was found in the urine while only 15% metabolism was detected in the feces sample at 2 h p.i. No efforts have been made to identify the metabolite(s).
biodist_description: [99mTc(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)] has the highest tumor uptake (7.30 ( 1.32 %ID/g) at 2 h p.i.the high kidney uptake and prolonged kidney retention of [99mTc-(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS)]

animal_model: Male severe combined immunodeficiency mice (C.B-17/Icr-scid Jcl) at 4 weeks of age.Models were used with AsPC-1 xenografts
metabolism: Rapid blood clearance
excretion: Mainly excreted through the kidneys
biodist_description: By replacing the connecting amino acid from glycine (Gly) to aspartic acid (Asp), the probe acquired a negative charge characteristic, significantly reducing its nonspecific accumulation in the liver and spleen. This structural modification did not affect the probe's ability to target and enrich tumors, thus [??Ga]CD6 successfully overcame the biodistribution defects of [??Ga]CG6. However, this probe still has two limitations: kidney retention issues, where radioactivity continues to accumulate in the kidneys at high levels; and a high accumulation trend in organs surrounding the pancreas (stomach and intestines).

animal_model: Injection of PC-3 tumor cells into the right anterior side of male athymic nude mice for modeling
excretion: Mainly excreted through the kidneys
biodist_description: The tracer was excreted mainly through the kidneys, as evidenced by the higher renal uptake at early time points and excretion via the bladder. At 120 min after injection, the tumor uptake of the tracer was higher than that in any other normal organ (Fig. 4b).

animal_model: Subcutaneous inoculation of BxPC-3 cells on the right anterior ventral side of female BALB/c nude mice
metabolism: We assessed the metabolic stability of 99mTc–HYNIC–cHK in normal BALB/c mice by analyzing blood and urine samples at 0.5 and 1h postinjection. Radio-HPLC chromatograms (Fig.2B–F) showed that the radiotracer remained intact in urine but underwent metabolism in blood, with degradation rates of 9.94% at 0.5h and 30.09% at 1h. Compared to the linear analog 99mTc–HYNIC–HK (Liu etal., 2014b), 99mTc–HYNIC–cHK exhibited significantly higher in vivo stability.
biodist_description: It possesses excellent targeting specificity; the T/M contrast is significantly enhanced through cyclization strategy; kidney-dominated excretion is the main limitation, but it can be optimized through subsequent chemical modification

For biodistribution studies, anesthetized U87MG tumor-bearing mice (n=3 for each group) were injected with 177LuDOTA-2.5D or 177Lu-DOTA-2.5F (1.28–2.17 MBq, 34.58– 58.76 μCi) via tail vein and sacrificed at 0.5, 2, 24, and 72 h after injection.

Radioactivity in each sample was measured using an automated gamma counter, and biodistribution was calculated as %ID/g.

animal_model: Athymic mice  bearing DU145 xenografts
metabolism:
The radioimmunoconjugate demonstrated high in vivo stability, with low uptake in liver and spleen, suggesting minimal metabolism or release of free
111
excretion: Clearance from blood followed a biphasic pattern. Low accumulation in kidneys suggests that renal excretion is not the primary route; overall clearance is likely mediated by systemic degradation and reticuloendothelial system processing.

animal_model: SCID mice​​ (male) bearing dual subcutaneous xenografts of ​​PSMA-positive PC3-PIP​​ cells.
metabolism: PSMA-mediated time-dependent internalization in LNCaP cells, high stability in human serum (>98% intact for 3h), and specific accumulation in PSMA(+) tumors with minimal metabolism in non-target tissues.
excretion: In SCID mice, most radioactivity is excreted into urine within 1h post-injection; high renal uptake (7.33±2.25 %ID/g) due to rodent proximal renal tubule PSMA expression, low liver accumulation (0.42±0.10 %ID/g).
biodist_description: The [⁶⁸Ga]Ga-T-SMDC showed specific and high uptake in PSMA-positive tumors (PC3-PIP) with significantly lower uptake in PSMA-negative control tumors (PC3-Flu) at 1 hour post-injection. animal_model: Female athymic Balb/c nude mice​​ bearing dual PC-3 PIP (PSMA+) and PC-3 flu (PSMA-) tumors.
cell_lines: PC-3 PIP (PSMA+) and PC-3 flu (PSMA-) cell
metabolism: Both compounds showed ​​high in vivo stability​​. Incubation in mouse serum at 37°C for 24h with an antioxidant left >96% of the compounds intact.
excretion: ​​[¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-PSMA-ALB-56​​ was ​​cleared much faster​​ from the blood and background organs (kidneys) than ​​[¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-PSMA-ALB-53​​, which showed prolonged circulation. Both compounds were cleared renally.
biodist_description: [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-PSMA-ALB-56 exhibited a more favorable tissue distribution profile than [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-PSMA-ALB-53. It achieved significantly higher tumor accumulation, much faster clearance from the blood pool and kidneys, resulting in superior tumor-to-background ratios across all measured organs (blood, kidney, liver). Uptake in PSMA-negative tumors was negligible, confirming high specificity.

animal_model: mice bearing PC-3 PIP and PC-3 flu tumors on the right and left shoulder
cell_lines: PC-3 PIP and PC-3 flu cell
metabolism: High PSMA-specific uptake in PIP tumors (76.4±2.5 %IA/g at 24h), fast blood clearance (<0.8 %IA/g at 24h), lowest renal retention (10.7±0.92 %IA/g at 24h), stable in saline (>95% intact at 4h) with L-ascorbic acid enhancing stability to ~97% at 24h.
excretion: Fast renal excretion (kidney uptake from ~60-67 %IA/g at 1h to 10.7±0.92 %IA/g at 24h), rapid blood clearance (<0.8 %IA/g at 24h), minimal retention in non-target tissues (liver, muscle, bone).
biodist_description: High PIP tumor uptake (76.4±2.5 %IA/g at 24h; AUC 6688±485 %IA/g·h), fast blood clearance (<0.8 %IA/g at 24h), lowest renal retention (10.7±0.92 %IA/g at 24h), high tumor-to-blood (176±27) and tumor-to-kidney (7.2±0.3) ratios at 24h.

animal_model: mice bearing BT-474 xenografts
metabolism: In BT-474 xenograft-bearing mice, 89ZrDFO-G3-DARPin shows rapid tumor uptake (visible at 1 h), retains in tumors up to 24 h, clears fast from circulation, and has dose-dependent kidney accumulation; it is stable in human serum for 4 h and formulation buffer for 24 h.
excretion: 89ZrDFO-G3-DARPin is rapidly excreted via the renal system; its effective half-life is 1.26 ± 0.84 h in the normal group and 0.32 ± 0.12 h in the blocking group, with dose-dependent kidney accumulation.
biodist_description: In BT-474 xenograft-bearing mice, 89ZrDFO-G3-DARPin shows specific uptake in tumors (4.41 ± 0.67 %ID/g at 24 h) with ~70% reduction in blocking group, rapid clearance from circulation, dose-dependent kidney accumulation, and low activity in other tissues.

animal_model: SCID mice-bearing tumor SKOV3cell line xenograft with HER2 overexpression
excretion: [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-DOTA-pertuzumab is cleared gradually from kidneys (5.6% ± 0.3% ID/g at 24 h p.i.) and liver (5.3% ± 0.25% ID/g at 24 h p.i.) over time, with slow blood clearance (12.9% ± 0.5% ID/g at 24 h to 1.6% ± 0.6% ID/g at 120 h p.i.) in SCID mice bearing SKOV3/SKBR3 xenografts.
